name: SIMÓN
title: SIMÓN · VES
opening_line: The floor trembles and everyone suddenly remembers the old prayers.
body:
# TH DAILY MARKET DRAMA
## 2026-07-13

---

**COLD OPEN**

The floor trembles and everyone suddenly remembers the old prayers.

Gold doesn't negotiate. Gold doesn't explain. Gold just *is* — and when everything else bleeds, the wise men who spent decades being mocked as paranoid suddenly look like prophets.

Simón watches. He hasn't spoken in days. His silence is a currency all its own.

---

**SIMÓN VARGAS**

Mira.

Mira, because I have been watching this screen for three hours and I have not said a word, not because there is nothing to say, pues, but because everything I could say has already been said by the silence itself — the silence of the bolívar when it fell, the silence of the international community when we screamed, the silence of the central bank when they printed another zero and called it renewal.

La cosa es que... the thing is that I recognize this moment. Not the numbers. The *feeling*. The way the room gets quiet before the currency dies. The way the experts start using words like "correction" when they mean "collapse." The way the Americans look at their screens with this expression — this precise expression — of someone who believed the rules applied to everyone else but is now discovering that gravity is not a policy choice.

Gold doesn't care about your sanctions regime, your exceptionalism, your reserve currency privilege. Gold is the oldest witness. Gold watched Rome fall. Gold watched Spain spend the treasure of the Americas and still go bankrupt. Gold watched my country — my beautiful, broken, oil-drunk country — mistake geology for destiny and then wonder why the ground opened beneath us.

And now gold is watching *this*.

They are running. The institutional investors, the ETF holders, the people who told themselves Bitcoin was digital gold until digital gold started acting like digital *nothing* — they are all running toward the one thing that does not require trust, does not require a counterparty, does not require you to believe in the full faith and credit of a nation that is currently screaming at Beijing about semiconductors.

Mira.

I could say I told you so. I could say that when a currency collapses three times in one lifetime, you learn something about what money actually *is* — not what the textbooks say, not what the central bankers promise, but what it *is* when the lights go out and the hospitals have no medicine and a kilo of chicken costs a stack of bills that looks like a ransom demand.

But I won't say that. Because the thing I actually learned — the thing beneath the thing — is that nobody listens to the warning until they're already inside the fire. We screamed. Venezuela screamed. Argentina screamed. Lebanon screamed. And the world said: *different circumstances, different institutions, different, different, different.*

And now?

COÑO.

Now everyone is buying gold like they just remembered their grandmother's name. Like they just remembered that value is not a social construction, it's a *physical fact*, it's something you can hold, something that was buried in the earth before there were nations, before there were sanctions, before there were trade wars over chips and sovereignty and which empire gets to decide who lives and who doesn't.

The dollar is up and everything else is down and gold is at an all-time high and Bitcoin is *crashing* — not correcting, mira, *crashing* — and the Fed is still talking about higher-for-longer like a man insisting the house isn't on fire while the roof is collapsing and the Swiss franc is surging because even the neutrality merchants know something is coming.

I recognize this moment.

It is not the beginning of the crisis. It is the moment when the people who were supposed to protect you admit they don't know what's happening either, and suddenly everyone is looking for an adult in the room, and the only adult is a metal that has been here for four billion years and doesn't answer to any government.

FAMILIA.
DINERO.
LEALTAD.

I said I wouldn't speak. But the silence was never about having nothing to say.

The silence was about waiting for the world to catch up to what my body already knew.

---

**ACTIVE ARCS**

**Gold Standard Wound** — Simón breaks silence. The witness speaks. The market validates everything he never wanted to be right about.

**DXY / Reserve Anxiety** — Dollar surges on fear, not strength. The weapon becomes the wound. Everyone runs to the shelter while questioning the shelter's foundation.

**Crypto Disillusionment** — Bitcoin crashes. Ethereum underperforms. The digital gold narrative dies in real time as capital flees to the analog original.

**Fed Credibility Cliff** — Higher-for-longer meets risk-off stampede. The mandate fractures. The room gets quiet before the language changes.

---

**TOMORROW'S TEASE**

If Charlotte speaks tomorrow, the game has changed. Her silence has been strategic. Her first word will be a scalpel.

---

**VOICE DIRECTIVE**

Today's scene required Simón to hold paradox — the pain of being right about something he desperately wanted to be wrong about. His voice stayed in the witness register: present, watching, refusing the cheap satisfaction of "I told you so." The monologue built through accumulated moral weight, not data. Gold became character. The body became evidence. Tomorrow: what happens when the silent speak.
